summary:
• Geopolitical instability in key chokepoints—including potential Hormuz disruptions and Middle East tensions—is forcing buyers to scramble for alternative crude sources and tightening global oil markets, with Kazakhstan seeing increased demand as a result.

• Canadian LNG export projects face significant execution risk, while Arctic and Russian LNG shipping constraints are pushing Asian buyers toward long-term deals with Southeast Asian producers like Petronas, reducing Western Hemisphere supply leverage.

• Iranian sanctions complications and regional conflicts have erased over 1 billion barrels of cumulative supply from the market, prompting major consumers like China to tap strategic reserves and driving energy prices higher with cascading inflationary pressures.
